title: CRS 001 — Capstone Use-Case Flows
subtitle: RAG tutor · review desk · session memory · ReAct agent

nodes.label: C01 Ingest
nodes.whyItMatters: Without ingest, RAG chat has nothing to retrieve.

nodes.label: C01 RAG Chat
nodes.definition: Retrieve top-k chunks → stuff context → prompt | llm → answer.
nodes.whyItMatters: Fixed RAG QA — same family as course RAG specialization.
nodes.safeSentence: Retrieve then generate; model does not pick the tool.

nodes.label: C02 Review Desk
nodes.definition: Sequential steps: sentiment → summary → customer reply → JSON.
nodes.whyItMatters: Multi-step without agent overhead when route is always the same.

nodes.label: C03 Session Memory
nodes.definition: session_id keys message history — load, append, save (chat_memory.json in dev).
nodes.whyItMatters: Same pattern as FastAPI stateful chat with Redis/DB in production.
nodes.commonTrap: One global list for all users.

nodes.label: C04 ReAct Agent
nodes.definition: LLM picks search_course_docs or calculator from tool descriptions each turn.
nodes.whyItMatters: Contrasts with fixed RAG — router is the model, not your if/else only.
nodes.commonTrap: Using make_watsonx_llm instead of make_watsonx_agent_llm for ReAct.

nodes.label: RAG as Tool
nodes.definition: search_course_docs wraps capstone 01 retrieval — agent invokes when needed.
nodes.whyItMatters: Composes capstone 1 + 4 — RAG is one capability among several.
